title: caladenia hirta subsp. hirta
intro: Caladenia hirta subsp. hirta, also known as the hairy spider orchid, is a type of flowering plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its distinctive greenish-yellow flowers with hairy sepals. Traditionally used in Indigenous Australian medicine, Caladenia hirta subsp. hirta is believed to possess medicinal properties that aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The plant's health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as reduce pain and discomfort. Caladenia hirta subsp. hirta can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical application, making it a versatile herbal remedy for a range of health concerns.
